Significantly decreases production. It won’t progress past the loading screen. Delete, reinstall, then it couldn’t find the preexisting printer. Delete, reinstall, then it says the printer doesn’t have scan function, which it does, when setting up a fax. I just scanned for a fax a few days ago after several attempts. Please stop adding unnecessary functions to this app (ads for added services/tools/printers etc) when the base functions aren’t consistently usable.

(I also have an HP Envy 5660 that just won’t quit. I’ll be donating it soon.) I’ve tried/used several other brands over the years, but none comes close to the ease of using an HP.  As stated setup is amazingly easy. Scanning is a breeze; the print quality is outstanding. Saving a document to a pdf from the print options is truly my favorite feature. Remote access is fantastic. title: Everyone Hates Printers, and it’s HP’s fault
content: Printers have always been hated, but they used to have more hardware issues than software. Every time my mom needs to print or scan something this software is the issue. Then after complying to that privacy invasion, I am told “printer not responding” “scanner unavailable” and the solution is to restart? Anyone involved in planning, coding, and/or promoting this app should go on strike, and then just quit, for the betterment of society. If you rated this app above 2 stars, please include how much they are paying you. I’m so relieved that my generation never uses scanners or printers, I am excited for this company to go bankrupt and die. Shout out to the movie Office Space, I hate everything about printers and it’s their fault.

author: Bleepless in Bellingham
title: Genuine HP ink cartridge not good enough for HP
content:
Bought a genuine HP ink cartridge, I used it to print a couple pages, and HP suddenly bricked it for being “HP Protected on another printer.” Clearly some sort of malfunction, but a pretty ridiculous “protection” feature, HP. And now I’ve lost half an hour troubleshooting this nonsense.

So, let’s get this straight—if I had purchased HP ink and my printer broke, and I bought another HP printer to use that same ink cartridge in (because the ink cartridges cost almost as much as a new printer, and why would I want to trash a brand new ink cartridge?), HP would refuse to use that cartridge because another HP printer had used it? That doesn’t “protect” anything but HP’s profits. 

Fellow consumers, when you think corporate greed, think HP. Gross way to treat your customers. Wasteful way to treat your product. Is it worth degrading yourself like this so you could pay your CEO $23 million dollars last year?

author: amjice
title: iOS App (Updated)
content:
App keeps getting better and better: the ability to select a 2-sided scan is great, thank you!

While the laptop version of the app is more robust, and understandably so,at I request that a 600 DPI scan control option be made available from within the app?

Firstly, the iOS app is far more convenient to control the scanner than it is to boot up the Windows laptop and use the more robust control interface.

Secondly, more and more travelers and security professionals recommend saving a digital copy in a folder structure in iOS and 600 DPI seems to be the norm for important documents like passports, driver licenses, birth certificates, etc. to capture the fine security details and other official imprints. 

It would only make sense, then, to use the iOS app and save directly into the iPhone / iPad than to separately boot up a laptop and then have to transfer it to the smartphone.

Thanks for your consideration of this important, often overlooked feature!

OLD: Would be great if the iOS Smart app can scan _both_sides_ from the document feeder like it already can using the Windows version of the app.  I can't imagine that would be a difficult feature to make available.

author: Sad I bought this 4
title: Scanning and Copy options
content:
Very disappointed. There is no way to scan double sided documents to email, or copy 2 sided to 2 sided documents. 
It was difficult to configure the scan to email settings which required many calls to tech support, both from my protection plan as well as HP. Both were unsuccessful. I used AI to do it myself. 
I wish I knew the scan and copy options before I bought this printer. If I had known I never would have purchased.

title: Endless hell loop
content:
Endless loop of malfunction if it doesn’t get the printer setup right the first time and then it keeps spamming you to sign up for ink service so you can’t fix whatever has gone wrong.

Great job guys!

author: Dee B Full
title: Severely Easy to Use
content: The fact that I can scan and send a document, pdf-ready on my phone is extremely convenient. I love this app. I can’t express how this solves those on-the-fly problems. Thank you!

author: Ywinters
title: Disgusted
content: I spent so much money on this HP computer printer you have to buy the app. You have to buy their paper and you have to buy their ink from them even when you’re not out of ink or paper and you can get the paper for a fraction of what they charge and then they charge you per seat for every copy over 50 pages so I end up owing them $50 a month after paying for the printer and I own it I should be able to make as many copies as I want stay away from these printers. I don’t care about the Quality. It’s all the other Scam that they’re doing to us consumers and you can’t get out out of it. You cannot use the printer or the scanner if you do not pay a monthly subscription, it does not

author: thjkjvc
title: App Doesn’t Connect
content: Spent a couple hours trying to get the app to recognize the printer in the dashboard. This Wahl calls with their tech-support team which will do everything they can or route you to their AI assistant. It’s a lot of work to actually get support anymore. Ultimately my printer never showed up in the dashboard. Returned to Costco.

author: Azpicky
title: Difficulty in print jobs
content: This printing process is unwieldy! Not user friendly. Too many steps. When I set this up it was a matter of making sure the Wi-Fi connection work and I could easily print with one basic click…however, today, I had to create an account, on the preview I couldn’t eliminated any pages I didn’t need..so I ended up with wasted pages and ink..I’m not happy with the many steps involved that weren’t there originally!
